content: Contrastive pre-training for zero-shot node generalization can be repurposed as the feature embedding backbone for a 2025 ICM curiosity autonomous agent, where unseen graph nodes that the contrastive pre-training cannot easily align to existing clusters are treated as intrinsically 'novel' states to drive exploratory behavior. This lets the agent automatically prioritize exploring out-of-distribution environments without manual reward shaping, while also improving the zero-shot generalization of the node embeddings through active exploration.
source_topics: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
trigger_topic: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
